title: nf-core/sopa pipeline parameters
description: Technology-invariant pipeline for spatial omics analysis that scales to millions of cells

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/sopa/usage#samplesheet-input).

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

spaceranger_options.title: Space Ranger options
spaceranger_options.description: Options related to Space Ranger execution and raw spatial data processing

spaceranger_probeset.description: Location of Space Ranger probeset file.

spaceranger_reference.description: Location of Space Ranger reference directory. May be packed as `tar.gz` file.
spaceranger_reference.help_text: Please see the [10x website](https://support.10xgenomics.com/spatial-gene-expression/software/downloads/latest) to download either of the supported human or mouse references. If not specified the GRCh38 human reference is automatically downladed and used.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).

reader.title: Reader
reader.description: Parameters related to the SpatialData reader

technology.description: Technology used for the spatial data, e.g., 'xenium', 'merscope', ...

visium_hd_imread_page.description: Optional page for the imageio reader

patches.title: Patches
patches.description: Creation of the image and transcript patches before segmentation

patch_width_pixel.description: Width (and height) of each patch in pixels

patch_overlap_pixel.description: Number of overlapping pixels between the patches. We advise to choose approximately twice the diameter of a cell

patch_width_microns.description: Width (and height) of each patch in microns

patch_overlap_microns.description: Number of overlapping microns between the patches. We advise to choose approximately twice the diameter of a cell

prior_shapes_key.description: Optional name of the boundaries element to use as a segmentation prior. Either a column name for the transcript dataframe, or a key of `sdata` containing the shapes names. If combining cellpose with proseg/baysor/comseg, it will be set automatically to `'cellpose_boundaries'`.

unassigned_value.description: If `prior_shapes_key` is provided, this is the value given to transcripts that are not inside any cell (if it's already 0, don't provide this argument)

tissue_segmentation.title: Tissue segmentation
tissue_segmentation.description: Parameters related to the tissue segmentation

level.description: Level of the image pyramid to use for tissue segmentation

mode.description: Mode for the tissue segmentation: 'staining' or 'saturation' (for H&E images).

tissue_segmentation_kwargs.description: Additional tissue segmentation parameters as a python dict string

filtering.title: Cell filtering
filtering.description: Filtering low-quality cells during and after segmentation

min_area_pixels2.description: Cells with an area less than this value will be filtered. The unit is in pixels^2, and used by Stardist/Cellpose.

min_area_microns2.description: Cells with an area less than this value will be filtered. The unit is in microns^2, and used by Baysor/Comseg. Not used by Proseg.

min_transcripts.description: Cells with less transcripts than this value will be filtered.

min_intensity_ratio.description: Cells whose mean channel intensity is less than `min_intensity_ratio * quantile_90` will be filtered.

aggregation.title: Aggregation
aggregation.description: Aggregation of genes and channels inside each cell

aggregate_genes.description: Whether to aggregate the genes (counts) inside each cell

aggregate_channels.description: Whether to aggregate the channels (intensity) inside each cell

expand_radius_ratio.description: Cells polygons will be expanded by `expand_radius_ratio * mean_radius` for channels averaging **only**. This help better aggregate boundary stainings

scanpy_preprocessing.title: Scanpy preprocessing
scanpy_preprocessing.description: Optional scanpy table preprocessing (log1p, UMAP, leiden clustering) after aggregation/annotation.

resolution.description: Resolution parameter for the leiden clustering

check_counts.description: Whether to check that adata.X contains counts

hvg.description: Whether to compute highly variable genes before computing the UMAP and clustering

explorer.title: Explorer
explorer.description: Parameters related to the Xenium Explorer visualization tool

pixel_size.description: Number of microns in a pixel. Invalid value can lead to inconsistent scales in the Explorer.

lazy.description: If `True`, will not load the full images in memory (except if the image memory is below `ram_threshold_gb`)

ram_threshold_gb.description: Threshold (in gygabytes) from which image can be loaded in memory.

image_preprocessing.title: Image preprocessing
image_preprocessing.description: Image preprocessing applied before channel-based segmentation methods (cellpose, stardist)

gaussian_sigma.description: Parameter for scipy gaussian_filter (applied before running the segmentation method)

clip_limit.description: Parameter for skimage.exposure.equalize_adapthist (applied before running the segmentation method)

clahe_kernel_size.description: Parameter for skimage.exposure.equalize_adapthist (applied before running the segmentation method)

proseg.title: Proseg
proseg.description: Parameters related to the proseg segmentation method

command_line_suffix.description: String suffix to add to the proseg command line. This can be used to add extra parameters to the proseg command line.

infer_presets.description: Whether to infer the proseg presets based on the columns of the transcripts dataframe.

visium_hd_prior_shapes_key.description: **Only for Visium HD data.** Key of `sdata` containing the prior cell boundaries. If `'auto'`, use the latest performed segmentation (e.g., stardist or the 10X Genomics segmentation). If combining stardist with proseg, it will be set automatically to `'stardist_boundaries'`.

comseg.title: Comseg
comseg.description: Parameters related to the comseg segmentation method

baysor.title: Baysor
baysor.description: Parameters related to the baysor segmentation method

cellpose.title: Cellpose
cellpose.description: Parameters related to the cellpose segmentation method

cellpose_channels.description: Channel name(s) to use for cellpose segmentation. If multiple, separate by space, comma or pipe characters.

cellpose_use_gpu.description: Whether to use GPU for Cellpose segmentation

cellpose_kwargs.description: Additional cellpose parameters as a python dict string

stardist.title: Stardist
stardist.description: Parameters related to the stardist segmentation method

stardist_model_type.description: Name of stardist model to use

stardist_channels.description: Optional channel name(s) to use for stardist segmentation. If multiple, separate by space, comma or pipe characters.

stardist_kwargs.description: Additional stardist parameters as a python dict string

tangram.title: Tangram
tangram.description: Parameters related to the tangram cell-type annotation method

use_tangram.description: Whether to run tangram cell-type annotation

sc_reference_path.description: Path to the scRNAseq annotated reference, as a `.h5ad` file

reference_preprocessing.description: Preprocessing method applied to the reference. Either None (raw counts), or `normalized` (sc.pp.normalize_total) or `log1p` (sc.pp.normalize_total and sc.pp.log1p)

bag_size.description: Number of cells in each bag of the spatial table. Low values will decrease the memory usage

max_obs_reference.description: Maximum samples to be considered in the reference for tangram. Low values will decrease the memory usage

fluorescence_annotation.title: Fluorescence annotation
fluorescence_annotation.description: Parameters related to the fluorescence-based cell-type annotation method

use_fluorescence_annotation.description: Whether to run cell-type annotation based on a marker-to-cell dictionary

marker_cell_dict.description: Dictionary mapping whose keys are marker channel names and values are the cell types associated to each marker. Should be provided as a string representation of a python dictionary.
